title: NOC 32104 – Animal Health Technologists and Veterinary Technicians | GTR Immigration
description: Animal Health Technologists and Veterinary Technicians under NOC 32104 play a vital role in animal healthcare services across Canada. With growth in companion animal ownership, livestock management, veterinary research, and public health initiatives, this TEER 2 occupation continues to provide employment opportunities nationwide. While labour market conditions vary by province, qualified and licensed professionals remain employable across veterinary clinics, research facilities, and agricultural sectors, making NOC 32104 a viable pathway toward Canada PR under select immigration streams.

classification.teer: Classification LevelDetailsBroad Occupation Category3 – Health occupationsTEER2 – College diploma or apprenticeship training of two or more yearsMajor Group32 – Technical occupations in healthSub-Major Group321 – Technical occupations in health (except practitioners of natural healing)Minor Group3210 – Technical occupations in therapy and assessmentNOC Code32104

duties: Animal Health Technologists and Veterinary Technicians perform some or all of the following duties:
Provide nursing care and rehabilitation therapy for animals
Restrain and monitor animals undergoing treatment or surgery
Produce radiographs and conduct laboratory tests
Assist veterinarians before, during, and after surgery
Prepare and administer medications and vaccines
Educate clients on nutrition and home care
employment_requirements: Completion of a two- or three-year animal health or veterinary technology college program
Successful completion of a national registration examination may be required
Registration with provincial veterinary technician or animal health technologist associations is mandatory in some provinces
Examples of provincial regulatory associations include:
Ordre des techniciens en santé animale du Québec
Internationally educated applicants must complete credential recognition and provincial registration where required.
salary_prospects: Annual earnings typically range from $28,900 to $53,900, depending on province, specialization, and experience.
job_outlook: The national outlook suggests moderate competition due to higher projected supply than openings. However, employment prospects remain favorable in:
Urban veterinary clinics with high pet ownership
Professionals with certification and specialization (anesthesia, dentistry, laboratory technology) may have improved employment prospects.
labour_market_demand: Animal Health Technologists and Veterinary Technicians are employed in:
Nationally, supply may exceed demand; however, regional shortages may occur in provinces such as Alberta, Saskatchewan, Manitoba, and rural parts of Ontario where agricultural services are essential.
Why Immigrate to Canada Under NOC 32104?
Animal Health Technologists and Veterinary Technicians benefit from:
Provincial Nominee Program opportunities in agriculture-focused provinces
Stable employment in veterinary and livestock sectors
Access to public healthcare and long-term settlement benefits
The veterinary services sector remains important to both urban pet care and Canada’s agricultural economy.
pathways: If you are a qualified Animal Health Technologist or Veterinary Technician, NOC 32104 may provide a pathway to permanent residence in Canada through selected immigration programs.
Eligibility assessment under Express Entry and PNP
